evidence: All 111 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored) — verified by running cargo test --lib contexts::milestone_record
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)

Amendment fr-19-b035a585 FIXED: matching_finalized_task_runs (model.rs:469-483) now gates the started_at fallback with entry.run_id.is_none() at line 481, so when the caller provides run_id=Some('run-3') and a finalized entry exists with run_id=Some('run-2') sharing the same started_at, the fallback skips it. Only runless terminal rows can match the fallback.

Amendment fr-19-b035a585 model test verified: matching_finalized_task_runs_ignores_different_named_run_at_same_started_at (model.rs:1145-1168) seeds a Succeeded entry with run_id='run-2', calls matching with run_id='run-3' at same started_at, and asserts empty result.

Amendment fr-19-b035a585 integration test verified: distinct_named_run_with_same_started_at_as_finalized_attempt_can_start (service.rs:3975-4046) appends a finalized entry with run_id='run-2', then successfully starts a new bead with run_id='run-3' at the same started_at — lineage has 2 entries, snapshot is Active with active_bead='bead-1'.

Amendment fr-19-b353e532 NOT APPLIED (planner rejected): The started_at fallback logic remains in same_attempt (model.rs:307-311), legacy_group_accepts_completion (model.rs:547-555), and find_matching_running_task_run (model.rs:416-443). This is correct — started_at is still the only correlation key for runless/legacy attempts.

Amendment fr-19-4993d634 FIXED: CompletionJournalDetails struct (fs.rs:2278-2289) now includes started_at: DateTime<Utc> as a required field. CompletionJournalDetails::merge (fs.rs:2296-2328) compares existing.started_at != requested.started_at at line 2300 before merging, so two different attempts completing at the same timestamp with different started_at values will NOT be merged.

Amendment fr-19-4993d634 verified: repairable_completion_event (fs.rs:2397-2420) delegates to CompletionJournalDetails::merge which checks started_at equality, so the repair path only activates for same-attempt replays.

Amendment fr-19-4993d634 integration test verified: completion_journal_dedupe_keeps_same_timestamp_attempts_separate (service.rs:2835-2903) creates two completion events for different attempts (started_at differs by 10 seconds) sharing the same finished_at/event timestamp, appends both via append_event_if_missing, and asserts exactly 2 distinct BeadCompleted events in the journal.
service.rs: All mutation paths (update_status, persist_plan, record_bead_start, update_task_run) run under with_milestone_write_lock for serialized access
service.rs: reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage rebuilds counters from latest_task_runs_per_bead, preserves Paused status, transitions Active→Ready when no active beads remain
All original acceptance criteria remain satisfied: milestone state answers 'what happened to bead X?' via find_runs_for_bead, controller can update linkage after each run outcome via update_task_run
findings_or_gaps: AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs ~line 549) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.

The outstanding risk — milestone mutations span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All paths are serialized by the milestone mutation lock and replay-safe, making this an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.

The active_bead_ids() function in model.rs still contains its own internal latest_task_runs_per_bead() call while reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage computes latest runs separately. External callers of active_bead_ids would redundantly recompute, but this is minor and not a correctness concern.
